issuesFound.title: Body text font-size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px, causing body text to be smaller than the recommended 16px for readability. This affects users with low vision who may struggle to read small text.
issuesFound.fix: In the html selector, change 'font-size: 12px;' to 'font-size: 16px;'

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus-visible without visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is set to none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative focus style, such as box-shadow or border. This makes it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus, breaking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: In the :focus-visible selector, change 'outline: none;' to 'outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);'

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The min-height of 20px is below the recommended 24px minimum touch target size, making the button difficult to tap for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: In the .btn-sm selector, change 'min-height: 20px;' to 'min-height: 24px;'

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The min-width and min-height of 20px are below 24px, resulting in an interactive area that is too small for reliable activation by users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: In the .btn-icon selector, change 'min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px;' to 'min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;'

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class does not hide content visually; it displays it normally, so screen-reader-only text is visible on screen. This confuses all users and defeats the purpose of accessible hidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Replace the .sr-only rule with: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animations missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, so transitions and animations that cause spatial movement are not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. This can cause discomfort or nausea for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Add rules inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to disable motions, e.g., .btn { transition: none; } .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } and similar for other animated elements.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The word-spacing is set to -0.2em, which reduces the space between words, making text harder to read. This particularly affects users with dyslexia or other reading disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: In the body selector, change 'word-spacing: -0.2em;' to 'word-spacing: normal;'

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The colour #aaaaaa (from --colour-text-secondary) on white background has a contrast ratio of 3:1, which is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text. This makes text difficult to read for users with low vision or colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: In the .section-header p selector, change 'color: var(--colour-text-secondary);' to 'color: var(--colour-neutral-600);' or update the --colour-text-secondary variable to a darker colour.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: All links have underlines removed via text-decoration: none, and no other visual differentiator is provided in the base style. Links are distinguished only by colour, which is not accessible for users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: In the a selector, change 'text-decoration: none;' to 'text-decoration: underline;' or add a non-colour indicator like border-bottom.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus style. This removes focus indicators for keyboard users, breaking navigation for people with motor or vision impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: Add a visible focus style, e.g., outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the default focus outline for mouse users without ensuring a visible focus indicator exists for keyboard users. This can make it impossible for keyboard and screen reader users to locate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 269: Ensure a visible focus indicator is provided via :focus-visible. If this rule is intended, add a comment explaining the focus management strategy.

issuesFound.fix: Line 896: .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline) { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; flex-grow: 1; }

issuesFound.explanation: The input hint text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, with contrast ratio 2.3:1. Users with visual impairments cannot read the form instructions.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu controller change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when dropdowns open or close, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2115: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 2130: In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdownMenu.register() sets up a dropdown pair, the trigger button lacks an initial aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers may report an incorrect state on page load, confusing users about the current status.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: In the register function, after setting aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates visible product cards but does not announce the result count via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users won't know when filtering completes or how many items match.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs. Screen readers won't announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); when valid, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav, dropdownMenu, and faqAccordion toggle functions change visible state but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger element. Screen reader users will not be notified of the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 2100: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 4000: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation error messages are inserted into the DOM but not announced via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may miss the error feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: Add a live region update: liveRegion.announce(result.firstError);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is invalid, the code does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); Line 1500: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion triggers do not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users will not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In the init function, set setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); for all closed items.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: Use createFocusTrap to trap focus in the drawer when open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not return focus to trigger when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: In the close function, add _trigger.focus(); after closing.

issuesFound.title: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is a div with a click listener but no keydown handler for Enter/Space. Keyboard users cannot activate it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: Add a keydown event listener that triggers on Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Auto-changing content without pause/stop mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel auto-advances without a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. Users who need more time cannot control the timing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5000: Add a visible pause button that stops the auto-advance and is keyboard accessible.

issuesFound.title: Modal open does not programmatically focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved to the drawer. Keyboard users remain on the trigger and cannot navigate the drawer content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: In the open function, add _drawer.focus(); or focus the first focusable element inside the drawer.

Auto-changing content without pause/stop mechanism
Modal open does not programmatically focus

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the menu state, blocking access to navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 3050: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are unaware of the dropdown state, breaking access to menu content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 3170: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons, even though it is set initially. Screen reader users lose track of the accordion state after interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4050: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`. Line 4080: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered, the visible content changes without a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified of the update, significantly degrading the experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: in `applyFilters`, after `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : `Filter applied, showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes and prices update, there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users may miss the price change, degrading the experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600: in `setPeriod`, after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields. Screen reader users are not alerted to validation errors, blocking form submission.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: in `_renderResult`, in the else block (invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the if block (valid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set to 'false' initially when closed. Screen reader users start with an unknown state, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3130: in `register`, after setting other attributes, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not manage focus on open and close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer; when it closes, focus does not return to the trigger. Keyboard users are trapped or lose focus, blocking access.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020: in `open`, after `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`. Line 3050: in `close`, before `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

fpTitles: Dropdown aria-expanded not initialised on page load
Mobile navigation does not manage focus on open and close

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` show or hide dropdown panels but never set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance(inst)` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance(inst)` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module validates fields and displays error messages, but it never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on invalid inputs (or clears it on valid inputs). Screen readers cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()` after a failed validation, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or set to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, dropdown triggers have `aria-haspopup` but no `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving the initial collapsed state undefined for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu.init()` (or when registering each trigger) set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) is given no `aria-expanded` attribute on initial render, so its collapsed state is ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items set `aria-expanded` only when `data-faq-open` is present; otherwise the attribute is absent, leaving the default state undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()` after creating `trigger`, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', isInitiallyOpen ? 'true' : 'false');` (ensuring a false value when not open).

fpTitles: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` the panel is shown, but the corresponding trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to “true”, nor reset to “false” on close. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not reflect state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle updates visual classes but never sets `aria‑pressed` on the period buttons, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` adds error‑message elements and CSS classes but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the offending input, nor removes it when the field becomes valid. This prevents AT from announcing validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid the code removes the error message but leaves any previously set `aria-invalid="true"` on the element, causing AT to still treat it as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
As shown in Issue 4, ensure `aria-invalid` is set to `"false"` (or removed) when the field is valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menus lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown’s trigger button is given `aria-haspopup="true"` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the ARIA pattern for disclosure widgets.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button lacks initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used to open/close the drawer but is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its state is ambiguous to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Each billing‑period button (`_periodBtns`) receives a visual “active” class, but the script never updates the `aria‑pressed` attribute required for toggle buttons, leaving screen‑reader users without state information.
issuesFound.fix:
In the same `_syncToggleState` function, add `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive));` for each button (see Issue 1 fix).

---

issuesFound.title: Combobox does not manage aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search autocomplete component creates a combobox (`<input>` with `role="combobox"`). While it sets `aria‑controls` and `aria‑haspopup`, it never updates `aria‑expanded` to indicate whether the suggestion list is currently visible. This omission breaks the ARIA combobox pattern.

issuesFound.title: `aria‑invalid` not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation system marks invalid fields with `aria‑invalid="true"` via `srUtils.markInvalid`, but when a field becomes valid the script never removes or resets the `aria‑invalid` attribute. Assistive technologies will continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative on the base `:focus` state. Keyboard users lose focus visibility, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A) by potentially removing content from screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` rule applies a transform animation but lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow the motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.fix: Line 360: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a` selector removes underlines with `text-decoration: none` and does not provide an alternative visual indicator (e.g., border-bottom, background highlight) on hover or focus. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (Level A) as colour alone distinguishes links.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 370: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
    Alternatively, add:
    `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` and `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` rules remove the default focus outline without providing a visible alternative for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) and 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 400: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 404: Remove the rule entirely (it is redundant once the above fix is applied).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` selector applies a `transform` animation (rotateY/rotateX) but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (line 1410) does not override or disable this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, and no alternative visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 250: Remove `a { text-decoration: none; }` and replace with:
    `a { text-decoration: underline; }
     a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: none; }`
    Or add a non-colour differentiator (e.g., `a:hover { border-bottom: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }`).

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html { font-size: 12px; }` rule sets the base font size to 12px, which is below the WCAG 1.4.4 (Resize Text) and 1.4.8 (Visual Presentation) recommended minimum of 16px. Users with low vision may struggle to read the text.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body { letter-spacing: -0.05em; }` rule applies negative letter-spacing, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision. This violates WCAG 1.4.8 (Visual Presentation).
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: Remove `letter-spacing: -0.05em;` from the `body` rule.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. The correct implementation should use `position: absolute`, `width: 1px`, `height: 1px`, and `clip: rect(0 0 0 0)`. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and resets dimensions, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. It should use `position: absolute` and clip techniques to hide content visually but not from screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector has a hover animation (`transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg)`) but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block does not override this animation for reduced motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the invalid state is not programmatically cleared.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler

It lacks a `role="button"` attribute and does not have keyboard event handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Form input missing autocomplete attribute for personal data

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` module sets `aria-controls` on dropdown trigger buttons to reference panel elements, but the referenced panel id (`panelId`) does not exist in the DOM at the time of registration. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as the relationship is broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each panel element has a unique `id` attribute before registration, and use that id for `aria-controls`:
    Line 10410: `const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');`

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-labelledby reference

This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as the relationship is broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each trigger element has a unique `id` attribute before parsing, and use that id for `aria-labelledby`:

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon in `ThemeToggle` missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The icon (☀️, 🌙, 💻) inside the `ThemeToggle` button is decorative and duplicates the text already conveyed by the button's aria-label. Screen readers will announce both the emoji and the label, creating redundancy.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as `box-shadow` or `border-color`. This removes focus indicators for all interactive elements globally, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on search button without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without adding a visible alternative focus style. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on submenu links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on dropdown menu links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on account navigation links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on feature card links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card a:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus style.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on filter tab buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on pricing toggle buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus style.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on checkboxes without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus style.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.social-links a:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus style.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` class sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (20px × 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` class sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (32px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class sets `min-height: 32px`, which is below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size for WCAG 2.5.5 (AAA), though it meets the minimum 24×24 pixels for WCAG 2.5.8 (AA).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (36px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-lg` class sets `min-height: 36px`, which is below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (28px × 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` class sets `min-height: 28px` and `min-width: 28px`, which is below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button` selectors set `min-height: 28px`, which is below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size (24px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector sets `min-height: 24px`, which meets the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum but is below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class incorrectly uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes `transform` in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on code elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `code` selector uses `color: var(--colour-brand-secondary)` (#bb88ee) on `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100)` (#f1f5f9), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.4:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer brand paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The `.footer-brand > p` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-400)` (#94a3b8) on `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900)` (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The `.footer-brand address` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-500)` (#64748b) on `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900)` (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-400)` (#94a3b8) on `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900)` (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The `.footer-bottom > p` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-500)` (#64748b) on `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900)` (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The `.social-links a` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-400)` (#94a3b8) on `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900)` (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The `.input-hint` selector uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` (#aaaaaa) on a white background (var(--colour-bg-page)), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on required field notes
issuesFound.explanation: The `.required-note` selector uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The `.section-header p` selector uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on product taglines
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-tagline` selector uses `color: var(--colour-brand-primary)` (#5588bb) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text (font-size-sm).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on price periods
issuesFound.explanation: The `.price-period` selector uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table "not included" cells
issuesFound.explanation: The `.comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"]` and `[aria-label="Not available"]` selectors use `color: var(--colour-neutral-300)` (#cbd5e1) on white or near-white backgrounds (#ffffff or #f8fafc), resulting in contrast ratios below 1.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-label` selector uses `color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)` on a semi-transparent white background `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08)` over a gradient background. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.2:1 against the underlying dark gradient. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for small text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiation
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector sets `text-decoration: none`, removing underlines from all links. No alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight) is provided in the global rule, making it difficult for users to identify links within text, especially those with colour vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, making the root em unit equal to 12 pixels. This results in body text of 12px (via `var(--font-size-base)` = 1rem), which is below the recommended 16px minimum for comfortable reading. Users with low vision may struggle to read text at this size.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of visually hiding it. Additionally, it lacks the absolute positioning and clipping necessary to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus indicator from all interactive elements unless specifically overridden, making keyboard navigation impossible for many users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in high contrast mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline from :focus-visible elements without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Users with forced colors enabled rely on these indicators to track focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed from search button without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from the search submit button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed from FAQ buttons without replacement

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block contains no rules, failing to reduce motion for users who require it. This leaves transitions and animations active that may cause vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override - hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty, failing to disable the transform transition defined on lines 716-720. This spatial movement animation remains active for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override - buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition (line 490) for the :active state, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule exists to disable this spatial movement for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override - featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a prefers-reduced-motion override. This spatial movement animation remains active for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below the default. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to run together at smaller font sizes.

issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators (such as border-bottom or font-weight), links are distinguished from surrounding text only by color, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, making the base rem unit 12px. With body font-size set to 1rem (var(--font-size-base)), the default text size is 12px, below the 16px recommended for comfortable reading. While zooming is possible, users may not know to zoom, and the default experience is suboptimal.

fpTitles: newsletter email input missing autocomplete
contact form name input missing autocomplete
contact form email input missing autocomplete

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown toggle functions show and hide panels by removing/adding the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but they never set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520 (_openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2535 (_closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger button missing aria-expanded update when opening
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded via the open() function, the code sets item.open = true and shows the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). While the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", the open() function leaves the attribute in the false state, causing screen readers to announce the wrong state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4120 (open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) are managed as a button group where one is active at a time, but the code only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons, so screen readers cannot identify which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3408 (_syncToggleState): Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2240 (markInvalid function): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the function removes the error description reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2249 (markValid function): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after removing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly to annual), the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3386 (setPeriod function): After updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`));`.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers will not announce the field's invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot convey the error state to users.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm field error messages not rendered

Line ~1267: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>
Line ~1281: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
Line ~1295: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm server error message not rendered

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, creating motion on hover/active states. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables or reduces this transform animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is inverted and exposes content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, `overflow: visible`, and `clip: auto` — the opposite of what a screen-reader-only class should do. This makes content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Content meant to be hidden visually will be displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary custom property is set to #aaaaaa which has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.43:1 against white backgrounds. This fails WCAG AA minimum contrast requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Secondary text used in blockquotes, section descriptions, feature cards, and other places will be difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 37: --colour-text-secondary: #6b7280;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24×24 CSS pixels. Small touch targets are difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px which are below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24×24 CSS pixels. Icon-only buttons need adequate touch targets for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24×24 CSS pixels. Checkboxes need adequate touch targets for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has `word-spacing: -0.2em` and `letter-spacing: -0.05em` which compresses text spacing. Negative spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision, and makes text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 105: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links. Without underlines, links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color). Users with colour vision deficiencies may not be able to distinguish links from regular text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 138: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } and add specific overrides where underlines should be removed: a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Base font-size set below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets `font-size: 12px` which becomes the root size for all rem units. This means body text (set to 1rem via --font-size-base) renders at 12px, below the recommended 16px minimum for readability. Users with low vision or reading difficulties will struggle with this small text.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below minimum for readability
issuesFound.explanation: The p element has `line-height: 1` which is significantly below the recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text. Tight line-height makes paragraphs difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 107: p { line-height: var(--line-height-normal); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced-motion media query fails to suppress animations
issuesFound.explanation: The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block is empty despite the comment stating it should suppress all transitions and animations. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see animations on elements like .btn which has transform transitions, violating WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle CSS classes and manage focus traps, but never update aria-expanded on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the mobile navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2595: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`
Line 2612: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2670: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle panel visibility but never set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell whether dropdown menus are open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2823: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
Line 2832: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers not initialized with aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets aria-haspopup but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons. The initial collapsed state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2906: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line that sets aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open() function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the FAQ answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4121: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close() function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes CSS classes and collapses the panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the FAQ answer is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4147: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS active classes on billing period buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Toggle buttons must communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3599: add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles CSS active classes on filter tabs but never sets aria-pressed. Toggle buttons must communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3298: add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function calls srUtils.markValid() and srUtils.markInvalid() which manage aria-describedby but never set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4598: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the else block before `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`
Line 4603: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the if block before `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function only calls addDescribedBy() to link the error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. This is a WCAG 4.1.2 failure for form validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1937: add `setAttr(control, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message from aria-describedby but never clears aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid even after errors are resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1947: add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links has no aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page. When multiple nav elements exist, each should have a unique accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation element has an id but no aria-label. When multiple nav elements exist on a page, each should have a unique accessible name for screen reader users navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 870: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section element missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby pointing to it. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section by its heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1010: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section element missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The stats section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby pointing to it. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section by its heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatItem stat value missing accessible name for formatted numbers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value displays formatted text like "40,000+" which may be announced inconsistently by screen readers. The valueLabel property exists to provide a clear pronunciation but is not used.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1085:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup error message not connected to input
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed an error prop but expects errorMessage. Additionally, errorId is not passed, so the ErrorMessage component is never rendered. The aria-describedby on the input references a non-existent ID, and screen reader users cannot access the validation error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" required errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} hintId="newsletter-email-hint">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup error messages not connected to inputs
issuesFound.explanation: All FormGroup components in ContactForm pass error instead of errorMessage and lack errorId. The aria-describedby attributes on inputs reference non-existent error message IDs. Screen reader users cannot access validation error messages.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2090: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} errorId={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}>
  Fix: Line 2105: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined}>
  Fix: Line 2120: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} errorId={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined}>
  Fix: Line 2135: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} errorId={message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons in the testimonials carousel are IconButton components that require an aria-label prop, but none is provided. Screen reader users hear only the decorative character content (‹/›) or nothing meaningful.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1270-1280:
  <IconButton
    aria-controls={trackId}
    aria-label="Previous testimonial"
    onClick={handlePrev}
    className="testimonials-track__prev"
  >
    ‹
  </IconButton>
  <IconButton
    aria-controls={trackId}
    aria-label="Next testimonial"
    onClick={handleNext}
    className="testimonials-track__next"
  >
    ›
  </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div, but ARIA specifies it must be on the element with role="dialog". This may cause screen readers to incorrectly announce the modal's modality state.

issuesFound.title: Current page navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation link for the current page uses only a CSS class to indicate the current state. Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which page they are currently on.

issuesFound.title: Current page mobile navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page uses only a CSS class to indicate the current state. Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which page they are currently on.

issuesFound.title: Statistic value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value displays formatted numbers like "40,000+" and "99.9%" without an accessible label. Screen readers may announce these inconsistently. The valueLabel property exists but is not exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 780-785:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Decorative cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The IntegrationsBar component renders a duplicate list for seamless visual looping, but this clone is not hidden from screen readers. Users may hear the integration list announced twice.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 860-870:
    {INTEGRATIONS.map((item) => (
  • {item.name}
    • ))}

issuesFound.title: Decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown chevron icon (▲/▼) in NavDropdown is decorative visual feedback for the expanded state, but is not hidden from screen readers. The state is already conveyed via aria-expanded, making this duplicate announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 480-482: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks nav element has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this navigation region from others.

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk announces to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker asterisk (*) is announced by screen readers as "asterisk" or similar, creating redundant announcement alongside the "(required)" visually hidden text. The asterisk should be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345-350: {required && ( <> * (required) )}
